programme.title: Biz Stream
programme.desc: , Episode 257. This episode focuses on how precision engineering and selective breeding are being used to reduce pollen and bring relief to millions of hay fever sufferers. Raja Pradhan, Phoebe Amoroso.

programme.title: Time-lapse Journey
programme.desc: . NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a variety of short programs focusing on Japan and Asia.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Hometown Stories
programme.desc: . On New Year's Eve, families in northeast Japan are visited by monster-like figures called namahage. We follow a group of men trying to keep up their proud tradition as the local population dwindles. Soness Stevens, Hiroyuki Hamada, Koji Okubo, Yusuke Ito.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The Factory Wonders
programme.desc: . Our team visits a heat-resistant glass factory in Koga, Ibaraki Pref., to witness the skills of the glassblowers and lampworkers, and learn about the secrets that heat-resistant glass holds.

programme.title: Time-lapse Japan
programme.desc: . Shimauta is the soul music of Amami Oshima. Islanders have long used it to convey feelings in good times and bad. The history of the music and of the island itself is conveyed in time-lapse footage.

programme.title: Newsline Asia 24
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Magical Japanese
programme.desc: . The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature, history, and culture. This episode looks at words related to foot and leg. Explore the unique culture behind them.

programme.title: Easy Japanese For Work
programme.desc: , Episode 17. #17 I've been traveling around Japan. / To say what you've been doing, change "masu" at the end of a verb to "te imasu." "Ryokoo-shimasu" (I travel) becomes "ryokoo-shite imasu" (I've been traveling).

programme.title: Culture Crossroads
programme.desc: . Is there a meal you'll never forget? People with international roots living in Japan take you on a tasty exploration of the dishes that brought timely emotional support in their lives.

programme.title: Hello! NHK World Japan
programme.desc: . Throughout April, NHK WORLD-JAPAN marks 10 years since the Kumamoto Earthquake by airing related programs. Discover efforts to restore Kumamoto Castle and visit the Minami-Aso Luna Observatory.

programme.title: Newsline Asia 24
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Seasoning The Seasons
programme.desc: . This program visits places across Japan to introduce the charms of their local daily life and festivals fostered by the nation's long history. Tomohiro Nakano.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Hometown Stories
programme.desc: . On New Year's Eve, families in northeast Japan are visited by monster-like figures called namahage. We follow a group of men trying to keep up their proud tradition as the local population dwindles. Soness Stevens, Hiroyuki Hamada, Koji Okubo, Yusuke Ito.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Journeys in Japan
programme.desc: Michael Keida explores the deep roots and enduring appeal of artisanal kokuto, the traditional sugar of the Okinawa islands.

programme.title: NHK NEWS 7
programme.desc: NEWS7 is NHK's flagship domestic evening news program, bringing you the latest from our Tokyo studio. It's presented with English audio on NHK World for our international viewers.

programme.title: The Factory Wonders
programme.desc: . Our team visits a heat-resistant glass factory in Koga, Ibaraki Pref., to witness the skills of the glassblowers and lampworkers, and learn about the secrets that heat-resistant glass holds.

programme.title: Time-lapse Japan
programme.desc: . Shimauta is the soul music of Amami Oshima. Islanders have long used it to convey feelings in good times and bad. The history of the music and of the island itself is conveyed in time-lapse footage.

programme.title: Newsroom Tokyo
programme.desc: . NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues. Yuko Fukushima, Aiko Doden, Takafumi Terui, Shoichiro Beppu, Hideki Nakayama, Takuma Yoshioka, Koichiro Tanaka, Chan-ju Kim, Abhishek Dhulia, Aki Shibuya.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Episode 241. Nail artist Kawajiri Mei is acclaimed by global fashion icons and artists for her boundless imagination and unique sense of humor. She tells us about the source of her inspiration. Gene Otani.

programme.title: Culture Crossroads
programme.desc: . Is there a meal you'll never forget? People with international roots living in Japan take you on a tasty exploration of the dishes that brought timely emotional support in their lives.

programme.title: Hello! NHK World Japan
programme.desc: . Throughout April, NHK WORLD-JAPAN marks 10 years since the Kumamoto Earthquake by airing related programs. Discover efforts to restore Kumamoto Castle and visit the Minami-Aso Luna Observatory.

programme.title: Newsroom Tokyo
programme.desc: . NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues. Yuko Fukushima, Aiko Doden, Takafumi Terui, Shoichiro Beppu, Hideki Nakayama, Takuma Yoshioka, Koichiro Tanaka, Chan-ju Kim, Abhishek Dhulia, Aki Shibuya.

programme.title: Dive in Tokyo
programme.desc: . Komagome is the birthplace of Somei-yoshino, the most popular cherry blossom variety in Japan. We visit a scenic garden, meet locals who cultivate cherry tree saplings, and explore shopping streets.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Seasoning The Seasons
programme.desc: . This program visits places across Japan to introduce the charms of their local daily life and festivals fostered by the nation's long history. Tomohiro Nakano.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Medical Frontiers
programme.desc: . Japanese researchers have been uncovering previously unknown effects of vitamin C in the body. We report on their latest findings, such as the power to strengthen the bones and prevent dementia. Erica Angyal, Maxwell Powers.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The Factory Wonders

programme.desc: . Shimauta is the soul music of Amami Oshima. Islanders have long used it to convey feelings in good times and bad. The history of the music and of the island itself is conveyed in time-lapse footage.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Hometown Stories
programme.desc: . On New Year's Eve, families in northeast Japan are visited by monster-like figures called namahage. We follow a group of men trying to keep up their proud tradition as the local population dwindles. Soness Stevens, Hiroyuki Hamada, Koji Okubo, Yusuke Ito.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Magical Japanese
programme.desc: . The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature, history, and culture. This episode looks at words related to foot and leg. Explore the unique culture behind them.

programme.title: Easy Japanese For Work
programme.desc: , Episode 17. #17 I've been traveling around Japan. / To say what you've been doing, change "masu" at the end of a verb to "te imasu." "Ryokoo-shimasu" (I travel) becomes "ryokoo-shite imasu" (I've been traveling).

programme.title: Culture Crossroads
programme.desc: . Is there a meal you'll never forget? People with international roots living in Japan take you on a tasty exploration of the dishes that brought timely emotional support in their lives.

programme.title: Hello! NHK World Japan
programme.desc: . Throughout April, NHK WORLD-JAPAN marks 10 years since the Kumamoto Earthquake by airing related programs. Discover efforts to restore Kumamoto Castle and visit the Minami-Aso Luna Observatory.

programme.title: Newsroom Tokyo
programme.desc: . NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues. Yuko Fukushima, Aiko Doden, Takafumi Terui, Shoichiro Beppu, Hideki Nakayama, Takuma Yoshioka, Koichiro Tanaka, Chan-ju Kim, Abhishek Dhulia, Aki Shibuya.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Episode 241. Nail artist Kawajiri Mei is acclaimed by global fashion icons and artists for her boundless imagination and unique sense of humor. She tells us about the source of her inspiration. Gene Otani.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: . Gojinjo Taiko is a wild, powerful performance by masked drummers. It originated 400 years ago, when villagers disguised themselves as oni to scare away a warlord's army.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: . We venture to the outskirts of Edo just after sunset, where a group of women and children have come to a stream to enjoy a popular summer activity. What could they be looking for among the weeds.

programme.title: Today's Close Up
programme.desc: . Japanese authorities are trying to recover thousands of toy guns that can fire live rounds with potentially lethal effect. NHK investigated how these dangerous products entered circulation. Hiroko Kuniya, Takeshige Morimoto.

programme.title: Seasoning The Seasons
programme.desc: . This program visits places across Japan to introduce the charms of their local daily life and festivals fostered by the nation's long history. Tomohiro Nakano.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Medical Frontiers
programme.desc: . Japanese researchers have been uncovering previously unknown effects of vitamin C in the body. We report on their latest findings, such as the power to strengthen the bones and prevent dementia. Erica Angyal, Maxwell Powers.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: BOSAI: Science that Can Save Your Life
programme.desc: . Tokyo Skytree, one of Japan's most famous tourist attractions, is equipped with a variety of countermeasures against disasters. We'll go behind the scenes to explore the tower's advanced technologies.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climates Change the World
programme.desc: . Episode 3 of this series on COP30 in Brazil looks at AMOC, an ocean current in the Atlantic. It transports warm water to the north, but melting ice threatens to stop it entirely.

programme.title: Time-lapse Japan
programme.desc: . Oku-Izumo is home to an ancient technique for making the steel used in Japanese swords. We meet a blacksmith who has inherited the tradition, and enjoy local scenery presented in time-lapse images.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Magical Japanese
programme.desc: . The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature, history, and culture. This episode looks at words related to foot and leg. Explore the unique culture behind them.

programme.desc: , Episode 17. #17 I've been traveling around Japan. / To say what you've been doing, change "masu" at the end of a verb to "te imasu." "Ryokoo-shimasu" (I travel) becomes "ryokoo-shite imasu" (I've been traveling).

programme.desc: . Is there a meal you'll never forget? People with international roots living in Japan take you on a tasty exploration of the dishes that brought timely emotional support in their lives.

programme.desc: . Throughout April, NHK WORLD-JAPAN marks 10 years since the Kumamoto Earthquake by airing related programs. Discover efforts to restore Kumamoto Castle and visit the Minami-Aso Luna Observatory.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Hometown Stories
programme.desc: . On New Year's Eve, families in northeast Japan are visited by monster-like figures called namahage. We follow a group of men trying to keep up their proud tradition as the local population dwindles. Soness Stevens, Hiroyuki Hamada, Koji Okubo, Yusuke Ito.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Journeys in Japan
programme.desc: In Hokkaido, Steven Lefever visits the ski resort town of Niseko to discover its many summertime attractions.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Dining With The Chef
programme.desc: . Chef Saito introduces two classic Japanese dishes often served in Japanese homes: pork ginger and sunomono. You can enjoy the taste of Japanese home cooking, wherever you are!. Tatsuo Saito, Rika Yukimasa, Yu Hayami, Patrick Harlan.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Document 72 Hours
programme.desc: . On Tsushima Island in southwestern Japan, a small store stocks fresh produce and everyday items and delivers to homes, restaurants, schools and more. Join us for three days on the island. Masako Nishijima.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Medical Frontiers
programme.desc: . Japanese researchers have been uncovering previously unknown effects of vitamin C in the body. We report on their latest findings, such as the power to strengthen the bones and prevent dementia. Erica Angyal, Maxwell Powers.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Activate Your Japanese!
programme.desc: . Learn how to make the most of your Japanese skills. Watch our drama that features useful communication strategies, our snappy videos about onomatopoeia, and our documentaries on "senpai" in Japan.

programme.title: Dig More Japan
programme.desc: . Instead of throwing away things that are old or broken, Japan fixes and reuses them, the culture of "mottainai." Let's dig a little deeper.

programme.title: Dining with the Chef mini
programme.desc: . Featured recipe: Buttered Mackerel with Ponzu/ Learn about Japanese home cooking with Master Chef Saito, based on traditional Japanese cooking techniques in five minutes.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Episode 242. Kadota Shunji has been working for many years to empower people with disabilities to live independently. His efforts have helped shape policies around the world. He talks about his journey so far. Gene Otani.

programme.desc: . The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature, history, and culture. This episode looks at words related to foot and leg. Explore the unique culture behind them.

programme.title: Science View
programme.desc: . This episode looks at leaf-cutter ants that grow food, and shake their bodies to communicate through vibrations. Plus, humans might grow spare teeth. Tomoko Tashiro, Naoko Yamazaki, Michelle Yamamoto, Yoichi Sato, Tomoko Tina Kimura, Rena Yamada, Ross Mihara, Rachel Walzer.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Dining With The Chef
programme.desc: . Chef Saito introduces two classic Japanese dishes often served in Japanese homes: pork ginger and sunomono. You can enjoy the taste of Japanese home cooking, wherever you are!. Tatsuo Saito, Rika Yukimasa, Yu Hayami, Patrick Harlan.

programme.title: Newsroom Tokyo
programme.desc: . NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues. Yuko Fukushima, Aiko Doden, Takafumi Terui, Shoichiro Beppu, Hideki Nakayama, Takuma Yoshioka, Koichiro Tanaka, Chan-ju Kim, Abhishek Dhulia, Aki Shibuya.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Episode 242. Kadota Shunji has been working for many years to empower people with disabilities to live independently. His efforts have helped shape policies around the world. He talks about his journey so far. Gene Otani.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: . Yoshida Ryusei, an October matsuri, features rockets called ryusei that are made by local teams and carry community prayers high in the sky. Team members pour heart and soul into their efforts.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: . Workers at a sawmill use the latest tools and their honed skills to prepare the building blocks of Edo life: long, narrow beams, bamboo stems and leftover wood stacked as high as buildings.

programme.title: Today's Close Up
programme.desc: . Are you always tired and struggle to sleep? You may be suffering from undernutrition. The condition even affects people who believe they eat balanced meals. We look at the causes and fixes. Hiroko Kuniya, Takeshige Morimoto.

programme.title: Document 72 Hours
programme.desc: . On Tsushima Island in southwestern Japan, a small store stocks fresh produce and everyday items and delivers to homes, restaurants, schools and more. Join us for three days on the island. Masako Nishijima.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Science View
programme.desc: . This episode looks at leaf-cutter ants that grow food, and shake their bodies to communicate through vibrations. Plus, humans might grow spare teeth. Tomoko Tashiro, Naoko Yamazaki, Michelle Yamamoto, Yoichi Sato, Tomoko Tina Kimura, Rena Yamada, Ross Mihara, Rachel Walzer.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Journeys in Japan
programme.desc: In Hokkaido, Steven Lefever visits the ski resort town of Niseko to discover its many summertime attractions.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.desc: . Chef Saito introduces two classic Japanese dishes often served in Japanese homes: pork ginger and sunomono. You can enjoy the taste of Japanese home cooking, wherever you are!. Tatsuo Saito, Rika Yukimasa, Yu Hayami, Patrick Harlan.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Activate Your Japanese!
programme.desc: . Learn how to make the most of your Japanese skills. Watch our drama that features useful communication strategies, our snappy videos about onomatopoeia, and our documentaries on "senpai" in Japan.

programme.title: Dig More Japan
programme.desc: . Instead of throwing away things that are old or broken, Japan fixes and reuses them, the culture of "mottainai." Let's dig a little deeper.

programme.title: Dining with the Chef mini
programme.desc: . Featured recipe: Buttered Mackerel with Ponzu/ Learn about Japanese home cooking with Master Chef Saito, based on traditional Japanese cooking techniques in five minutes.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Dive in Tokyo
programme.desc: . Join us as we explore Sendagaya, an area nestled between Shinjuku and Harajuku. It's home to sports facilities, venues for Noh theater and the board game shogi, and hundreds of apparel makers.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Episode 265. Ichiko Aoba puts distinctive, whispery vocals over floating, classical guitar melodies. We explore why her music captivates listeners around the world, despite being sung in Japanese. Gene Otani.

programme.title: Japan's Top Inventions
programme.desc: . Documentary looking at how Japanese craftsmanship has created inventions that are now used and loved around the world. Peter Von Gomm, Yuki Rhinehart, Jason David Danielson, Koki Ando, Toru Iwatani, Shigetaka Kurita, Yoshihiko Akimoto.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Frontrunners
programme.desc: . Fukuoka's summer festival, Hakata Gion Yamakasa, features huge floats decorated with elaborate dolls. We meet Nakamura Hiromine, a fourth-generation doll maker fusing this tradition with modern ideas.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Science View
programme.desc: . This episode looks at leaf-cutter ants that grow food, and shake their bodies to communicate through vibrations. Plus, humans might grow spare teeth. Tomoko Tashiro, Naoko Yamazaki, Michelle Yamamoto, Yoichi Sato, Tomoko Tina Kimura, Rena Yamada, Ross Mihara, Rachel Walzer.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.
